sentence_reviews.text: Syntax, semantics, intermediate representations, compilation, and types structure executable reasoning and software tools.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 176-180, 205-221, 248-253, and 776-779; Intermediate representation page definition and intermediate language sections; Types and Programming Languages page subject/description
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports syntax, semantics, logic programming, and language-definition material; the IR source supports compiler intermediate representations; the type-systems source supports type systems as a programming-language theory topic. The 'executable reasoning and software tools' phrasing is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Discrete mathematics & logic, Algorithms & data structures.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Programming languages, compilers & type systems rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Discrete mathematics & logic.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 173-199, 205-221, and 776-779; Types and Programming Languages page subject/description
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support logic syntax/semantics, BNF language definitions, and type systems/programming-language theory. The exact 'rests on' dependency wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Programming languages, compilers & type systems rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Algorithms & data structures.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 75-79 and 776-779; Intermediate representation page definition and examples sections
sentence_reviews.notes: Compilers and IRs process programs using algorithms and data structures, and AIMA explicitly includes algorithms and pseudocode. The source support is indirect but defensible.

edge_reviews.notes: Syntax, semantics, formal logic, and type systems make discrete math and logic a defensible core prerequisite.

Compiler and IR processing are algorithmic and data-structure-heavy; AIMA also links algorithms and language/pseudocode material. Core strength is defensible for compilers, though less directly for type theory alone.
